last_item: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler UI slice 2/3 — enable endpoint POST /api/pooler/enable (internal/server/handlers_pooler.go handlePoolerEnable). Wires the already-built/tested pgbouncer.Manager.Enable orchestrator into HTTP. New pooler *pgbouncer.Manager field built in newServer over the shared OS runner (s.pg as VerifierSource, s.store as PoolerState). Under requireAuth → inherits CSRF gate for cookie flows; no exempt entry so band-1 TestEveryStateChangingEndpointRejectsCSRF route-walk auto-covers it. Input {roles, profile} ONLY — max_connections is NOT client-supplied; pool sized server-side from live s.pg.CurrentTuning().Applied.MaxConnections (security tie-break: forged value can't widen pool past what PG serves). Guard order cheap→costly: empty roles→400 (empty auth_file locks every app out), unknown profile→400 (ParseWorkloadProfile no silent mis-size), then Applied==nil (PG unreachable)→409 CodeConflict with NO side effect rather than guess-then-install. Audited enable_pooler success/failure by CODE only (never role names/verifiers; Enable itself logs role COUNT only). Tests handlers_pooler_test.go: requires-auth(401,flag-stays-off), empty-roles-400, unknown-profile-400, refuses-when-PG-unreachable-409(flag-stays-off). Full side-effecting happy path NOT HTTP-tested by deliberate convention (backups/migrate handlers same: real OSRunner, test only guard/validation) — orchestration proven in pgbouncer/enable_test.go via fake runner. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no high-confidence findings. REMAINING: slice 3/3 React UI pooler toggle.
_unused_prev: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler UI slice 1/3 — read-only status endpoint GET /api/pooler (internal/server/handlers_pooler.go), behind requireAuth, returns {enabled, host, listen_port, pool}. enabled from pgbouncer.IsEnabled (config key pooler.enabled; absent=default-off); host/listen_port the loopback target same-box apps use (new exported pgbouncer.LoopbackHost/DefaultListenPort aliasing the unexported consts, one source of truth); pool the host-sized RecommendPool(max_connections, Mixed) sizing enabling would apply so the UI labels each setting by effect. Pool best-effort via s.pg.CurrentTuning — Postgres unreachable (Applied==nil) -> Pool nil, UI says computed-at-enable-time, never a guess. NEVER mutates host state; tuning-read error non-fatal so enabled still renders. Split the old single 'UI toggle' backlog item into 3 slices (this status endpoint done; future POST enable endpoint; React UI). Tests handlers_pooler_test.go: default-off, reflects-persisted-enable (reads same key Enable writes), requires-auth (401, no SPA leak). Dropped a premature unused pooler *pgbouncer.Manager Server field (YAGNI). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no critical/blocking; declined the one Important finding (test CurrentTuning-error branch) — currently unreachable, would need an injection seam nothing else uses.
_prev_last_item: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler enable-flow slice 7 — backend ORCHESTRATOR. internal/pgbouncer/enable.go Manager.Enable(ctx, src VerifierSource, state PoolerState, p EnableParams) (EnableResult, error) wires the six already-built primitives into one opt-in entry point. OFF by default; idempotent/re-runnable. Order: InstallPackage -> RoleVerifiers (src, satisfied by *pg.Manager) -> EnsureConfig -> EnsureUserlist -> EnableNow -> Reload ONLY when config/auth_file changed -> IsRunning verify -> persist enabled=true LAST (only once confirmed up, so the flag can't contradict a failed bring-up). Added IsEnabled (unset key=default-off) for the UI slice; PoolerState is a narrow GetConfig/SetConfig iface *store.Store satisfies (no store coupling into the package). SECURITY (reviewer-caught, fixed pre-commit): EnsureConfig BEFORE EnsureUserlist — the .ini marker guard is the flow's only ownership check, so the secret-adjacent auth_file is never written into a dir indiepg doesn't own; a foreign distro/operator pgbouncer.ini is a hard stop with no auth_file left behind. Logs role COUNT only, never names/verifiers. Tests enable_test.go via fake VerifierSource + in-memory PoolerState + temp confdir (10 Enable cases + IsEnabled). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): both findings applied (ordering swap + bring-up-failure test hardening). REMAINING: UI pooler toggle slice.
last_result: green: gofmt clean, go vet ok, go test ./... pass, CGO_ENABLED=0 build ok (web untouched; UI toggle is slice 3/3)
notes: BANDS 1.5 + 2 + 2.5 COMPLETE; BAND 3 (usability) IN PROGRESS. Provision-flow item (a) DROPPED iter 33 (no panel provision route — CLI install; served by the tuning card). The ONLY remaining band-3 feature is the OPT-IN PgBouncer pooler, now being built incrementally like RecommendTuning was. PgBouncer slice 1 DONE iter 33: pure host-sized pool math internal/pgbouncer/pool.go (RecommendPool + SettingsMap, ports sm/DEFAULTS.md: avail=max_conn-5, default_pool=int(avail*util) floor20, min=/4 floor5, reserve=/5 floor5, max_client=default*multiplex, idle 300/600; safe defaults scram/transaction/DISCARD-ALL hardcoded). NEXT band-3 sub-items (in order): (i) DONE iter 34 — pgbouncer.ini render internal/pgbouncer/config.go RenderConfig; (ii) DONE iter 35 — config installer internal/pgbouncer/install.go Manager.EnsureConfig (atomic temp+rename, owner pgbouncer 0640, marker-guarded foreign->CodeConflict, deterministic no-op, O_NOFOLLOW symlink guard); (iii-a) DONE iter 36 — SCRAM userlist render internal/pgbouncer/userlist.go RenderUserlist([]UserlistEntry) (pure render of the `"user" "verifier"` auth_file: >=1 entry, SCRAM-only/no-downgrade, verifier+username injection guards, dup-refused, deterministic sort); (iii-b) DONE iter 37 — atomic auth_file installer internal/pgbouncer/install.go Manager.EnsureUserlist(ctx, []UserlistEntry) (renders via RenderUserlist then installs userlist.txt atomically: temp+rename, 0640 owner pgbouncer, O_NOFOLLOW symlink guard, deterministic no-op; NO marker guard since the format can't carry one — ownership gated upstream by EnsureConfig's .ini marker, auth_file is a derived satellite; logs only user count; DRYed shared atomicInstall(path,data,mode) out of writeConfigFile); (iii-c) DONE iter 38 — pg_authid SCRAM verifier query internal/pg/verifiers.go Manager.RoleVerifiers(ctx, roleNames []string) ([]RoleVerifier, err): the one input EnsureUserlist/RenderUserlist still needed fed, read via the PRIVILEGED psql path (rolpassword is superuser-only) — logs nothing, ValidateIdentifier+QuoteLiteral names, missing-role NotFound, empty-rolpassword Validation (never dropped), de-dup, deterministic sort, verbatim pass-through (SCRAM gate stays in RenderUserlist); (iii-d) DONE iter 39 — service-lifecycle primitives internal/pgbouncer/service.go: InstallPackage (apt update+install -y pgbouncer, noninteractive, idempotent, update-fail-stops-install), EnableNow (systemctl enable --now, idempotent), Reload (systemctl reload/SIGHUP first — no client-conn drop — restart fallback; both-fail CodeExec; caller reloads ONLY when changed), IsRunning (is-active: reported-state→false, empty-stdout+runner-error→CodeExec so verify-after-start can't misread 'couldn't ask' as 'down'). NOT wired yet. (iii-e) <== NEXT orchestrator Enable(...): InstallPackage -> RoleVerifiers -> EnsureUserlist + EnsureConfig -> EnableNow -> Reload ONLY when config/userlist changed -> IsRunning verify; OFF by default; idempotent; persist enabled state; any restart follows sm safety conventions; (iv) UI toggle (off by default) showing the app connection string via the pooler + pool settings labeled by effect, with a confirm stating exactly what enabling does + vitest test. After these, band 3 is complete -> band 4 (UI redo, shadcn). DONE iter 32: destructive-confirm AUDIT + TESTS — all confirm sites already state consequence + irreversibility and gate destructive ops behind a typed exact-name match; was untested, so added web/src/components/ConfirmDialog.test.tsx (8 tests) locking: plain-language consequence shown, TypedConfirmDialog cannot fire onConfirm until exact name typed (also gated while busy). No component changes. Band-3 item (b) DONE. Test-only tip: web npm/npx from repo ROOT hits a read-only ~/.npm cache (EROFS) when it tries to fetch — run the web gate from web/ (npm run typecheck/build/test) or invoke web/node_modules/.bin/{vitest,tsc} directly with the project path. NEXT IS BAND 3 (usability): top open items — (a) provision flow shows computed best-defaults up front with each override labeled by effect, safe to accept blindly; (c) PgBouncer opt-in pooler toggle (DEFAULTS.md pool math, off by default, shows app connection string). NOTE the tuning UI just shipped is read-only/preview; if a real in-panel profile-switch is ever wanted it MUST funnel through ApplyTuning's restartWithRollback + a confirm dialog stating the brief restart (fits band-3 destructive-confirm item) — but it is NOT load-bearing for the north star, so only add it if usability work surfaces a genuine need. DONE iter 31: APPLY host-sized tuning UI SURFACE — read-only GET /api/tuning (Manager.CurrentTuning, internal/pg/tuning_status.go) returns detected RAM/CPU + live applied settings (from pg_settings, whole MB, Applied=nil best-effort when PG unreachable) + per-profile host-sized recommendation; 'Database tuning' card on Settings (web/src/views/DatabaseTuning.tsx) shows applied settings + a per-profile PREVIEW labeled by effect, with copy making clear a non-default selection changes nothing and a real switch needs a restart (no restart-trigger surface). DONE iter 30: APPLY host-sized tuning REAL-PG INTEGRATION TEST (internal/pg/tuning_apply_integration_test.go, //go:build integration, INDIEPG_PG_BINDIR-gated). Proves ApplyTuning end-to-end on a throwaway PG14 cluster: settings land in pg_settings (bytes via unit col, max_connections exact), 2nd run no-op, an unstartable max_connections=1 rolls back via restartWithRollback with PG up -> CodeSafety. tuningTestRunner translates the exact `systemctl restart postgresql` -> pg_ctl restart. INTEGRATION-TEST GOTCHAS: force a deterministic boot failure with a CROSS-GUC constraint (max_connections=1), NOT an oversized memory GUC (overcommit makes it hang); and pg_ctl start/restart needs -l <logfile> or the daemonized postmaster holds the captured stdout pipe and cmd.Run hangs forever. ONLY REMAINING band-2.5 item: the ApplyTuning UI SURFACE — show the applied host-sized defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect (oltp/olap/mixed); vitest/RTL test; off-the-default profile clearly described before it changes sizing. After that, band 2.5 is complete -> band 3 (usability). DONE iter 29: APPLY host-sized tuning at provision — Manager.ApplyTuning (internal/pg/tuning_apply.go) persists RecommendTuning's five settings via ALTER SYSTEM, idempotent vs pg_settings (bytes-normalised through the unit col), restart-requiring (shared_buffers/max_connections) funneled through restartWithRollback, reloadable via pg_reload_conf. Wired into Provision (replaces iter-28 'surfaced, not applied'); a CodeSafety rollback is tolerated gracefully (provision still succeeds on prior good config). detectTuning seam added for deterministic provision tests. Fully unit-tested. THE TWO NEW TOP BAND-2.5 ITEMS are the apply follow-ups: (a) a real-PG //go:build integration test proving settings land in pg_settings, a 2nd run no-ops, and an oversized shared_buffers rolls back with PG up; (b) the UI surface showing the applied defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect. ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — ApplyTuning is the worked example. DONE iter 28: host-sized tuning PURE SIZING FUNCTION — RecommendTuning(memoryMB, cpuCount, profile) in internal/pg/tuning.go ports the sm tuning.py math (DEFAULTS.md) for shared_buffers/effective_cache_size/work_mem/maintenance_work_mem/max_connections; WorkloadProfile selectable, empty->Mixed default, unknown->ValidationError; total/panic-free on degenerate input. Tested vs sample RAM 1GB-128GB x all 3 profiles + clamp/ordering/degenerate/parse/SettingsMap. DONE iter 27: connection-saturation alert CRITICAL tier connections-critical (pg.connections_percent >= 95%, For 1m, Cooldown 15m, Critical) escalating the existing connections-near-max WARNING (85%); rule-only, metric already real (sampler.go pg_stat_activity / max_connections), ID-based seed auto-adds on upgrade; TestConnectionSaturationTiers proves both tiers fire non-vacuously. DONE iter 26: SELF-HEALING config primitive (internal/pg/safeconfig.go): snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable); restartWithRollback restarts PG and on non-zero `systemctl restart` exit restores the snapshot via `tee` as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) + restarts again so PG is never left down -> CodeSafety ('rolled back; PG running') or CodeInternal ('PG down'+hint). ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — this is exactly what the new APPLY item above must do. DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% host.disk_percent) below critical disk-almost-full (90%). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY — Snapshot already has the fields, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes the percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test; background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically. --- Alert plumbing: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
